picks.rationale: PANW is up 94% YTD and 73% over 1 year, with strong 1M momentum of +24.12% and positive news sentiment of 0.147, driven by the new IBM–Red Hat security alliance and Jim Cramer's buy call [simplywall.st](https://simplywall.st/stocks/us/software/nasdaq-panw/palo-alto-networks/news/palo-alto-networks-panw-is-up-144-after-new-ibmred-hat-secur) [insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/palo-alto-networks-panw-shares-up-significantly-after-jim-cramer-said-they-were-a-buy-1795911/?amp=1).

CRWD has surged 71% YTD and 54% over 1 year, with strong 1W momentum of +14.33% and a 52-week high, supported by a sector rotation into cybersecurity and AI-driven demand [tikr.com](https://www.tikr.com/blog/palo-alto-networks-just-hit-a-record-high-at-85x-earnings-heres-where-the-stock-could-go-in-2026).

GOOGL is up 104% over 1 year and 14.35% YTD, with a reasonable P/E of 27.47, strong buy analyst consensus (score 1.44), and low short interest of 1.53%, reflecting robust fundamentals and AI-driven growth.
SNOW has gained 20% YTD and 15% over 1 year, with strong 1W momentum of +14.57% and positive news sentiment of 0.246, supported by geographic expansion and strong buy analyst consensus (score 1.49).
ASML is up 125% over 1 year and 52.57% YTD, with a P/E of 60.24, strong buy consensus (score 1.44), and very low short interest of 0.43%, driven by a soaring order backlog and wafer market expansion.
AMAT has surged 216% over 1 year and 124.85% YTD, with strong 1M momentum of +20.42% and a strong buy analyst consensus (score 1.49), benefiting from semiconductor equipment demand.

UBER has a P/E of 18.47, strong buy consensus (score 1.48), positive news sentiment of 0.168, and low beta of 1.113, offering value with upside from autonomous driving partnerships like AMD-backed Turing.
FTNT is up 100.63% YTD and 47% over 1 year, with 1M momentum of +6.67% and a P/E of 60.56, benefiting from AI security spotlight despite a hold analyst consensus (score 2.75).
SHOP has 1M momentum of +5.77% and 1W momentum of +7.02%, with a market cap of $155B and positive news sentiment of 0.025, though P/E is elevated at 117.12.
INTC has surged 447% over 1 year and 205.61% YTD, with 1M momentum of +6.78% and positive news sentiment of 0.141, but carries a hold analyst consensus (score 2.63) and no P/E due to losses.
